fields.Name: Neon CRM
fields.Description: Neon CRM (Neon One) connector — syncs accounts, donations, memberships, events, registrations, campaigns, activities, grants, orders, payments, pledges, volunteers, and custom objects via the Neon REST API v2 (HTTP Basic auth).

Configuration.AuthNotes: HTTP Basic Auth: org ID as username, API key as password. Base64-encode orgId:apiKey and prefix with Basic. NEON-API-VERSION request header should be included with each call (e.g. 2.11). OAuth2 auth-code flow is present in the OAS3 spec (authorizationCode grant with scopes 1-10) but the developer documentation explicitly states Neon CRM does not currently support OAuth for system users — OAuth is for constituent (end-user) authentication only. Connector must use HTTP Basic for all API v2 calls.
Configuration.APIVersioningNotes: NEON-API-VERSION request header. Omitting defaults to latest version. Requests specifying a deprecated or invalid version receive a 4XX response. Older versions are deprecated over time with advance notice.

PaginationDefaults.notes: Offset page-number pagination. PageParam currentPage (0-indexed per PageParam schema: minimum 0, default 0). pageSize default 20, maximum 200. Search endpoints (POST /*/search) embed pagination in the SearchRequest body under pagination field. List endpoints accept currentPage and pageSize as query params. Response includes pagination envelope with currentPage, pageSize, totalPages, totalResults.

IncrementalSyncCapability.notes: Neon CRM v2 does not expose a dedicated modifiedSince query parameter on list/search endpoints. The SearchCriteria operator set includes GREATER_AND_EQUAL and GREATER_THAN operators. Records carry a timestamps.lastModifiedDateTime field (from the Timestamps schema: createdBy, createdDateTime, lastModifiedBy, lastModifiedDateTime). Incremental sync is achievable via POST search with a SearchCriteria on lastModifiedDateTime GREATER_AND_EQUAL watermark for objects that support the search endpoint. Grants specifically expose top-level lastModifiedDate and createdDate fields. Objects without search endpoints require full re-fetch or content-hash comparison. The IncrementalWatermarkField per IO will be set to lastModifiedDateTime (via timestamps sub-object) where supported.

Configuration.WebhooksNotes: Webhook subscriptions managed via CRUD on /webhooks (POST, GET, PUT, DELETE, PATCH). Triggers: CREATE_ACCOUNT, UPDATE_ACCOUNT, DELETE_ACCOUNT, CREATE_DONATION, UPDATE_DONATION, DELETE_DONATION, CREATE_MEMBERSHIP, UPDATE_MEMBERSHIP, DELETE_MEMBERSHIP, CREATE_ACTIVITY, UPDATE_ACTIVITY, DELETE_ACTIVITY, CREATE_EVENT_REGISTRATION, UPDATE_EVENT_REGISTRATION, DELETE_EVENT_REGISTRATION, CREATE_ORDER, UPDATE_ORDER, DELETE_ORDER, CREATE_MERGED_ACCOUNT. Webhook delivery payload schemas are documented at developer.neoncrm.com/webhooks/ but NOT in the OAS3 spec. Delivery payload is out of scope for this connector. Webhook subscription management (the /webhooks CRUD surface) is in scope.
Configuration.BulkOperationsNotes: No bulk/batch endpoints in the OAS3 spec v2.11. All operations are single-record. POST /*/search provides filtered multi-record retrieval but is not a batch write endpoint.

universalPK.notes: The Neon CRM API v2 uses id as the universal PK field name across most resource types (accounts use accountId, events use id, registrations use registrationId). The primary pattern across the OAS3 spec path parameters is {id}. Runtime D4 SoftPKClassifier will validate per-IO.

WriteCapability.notes: Full CRUD is available for most major resources (Accounts, Donations, Events, EventRegistrations, Memberships, Activities, Grants, Campaigns, Pledges, Recurring Donations, Webhooks). OnlineStore (catalog, categories, products) is read-only (GET only). Properties tag is read-only. Some sub-resources are read-only. The OAS3 spec defines POST (create), PUT/PATCH (update), and DELETE operations across 34 tags.

Configuration.ConcurrencyControlNotes: The OAS3 spec v2.11 defines no ETag, If-Match, If-Unmodified-Since, or version-number fields on any resource. No optimistic concurrency headers are declared. Conflict resolution must use snapshot-3-way only.
Configuration.DeleteSemanticsNotes: DELETE operations return 200 with an id result or empty object — no soft-delete / archive-flag pattern is documented in the OAS3 spec. DELETE is treated as hard delete. No tombstone mechanism is documented for deleted records, which means orphan records in the destination cannot be detected from the API alone. Webhook triggers for DELETE_ACCOUNT, DELETE_DONATION, etc. exist but require a live webhook subscription.
Configuration.DefaultSyncDirectionReasoning: Read-only (Pull) by default per policy. Write-path (bidirectional) requires sandbox validation before enabling against a client system. No ETag/version concurrency control means conflict resolution is snapshot-3-way only.
Configuration.DefaultConflictResolutionReasoning: No ETag or version field — MostRecent is not reliably safe. DestWins is the conservative default when bidirectional is eventually enabled.

ErrorResponseShape.successResponseShape: HTTP 200/201 with resource object or empty content
ErrorResponseShape.errorResponseShape: Array of APIMessage objects: [{ code: string, message: string }]. Empty content ({}) for 401, 403, 404 on most endpoints. Array<APIMessage> for 400 responses on newer endpoints (Grants, Volunteers, Custom Objects). 429 Too Many Requests with no documented body schema.
ErrorResponseShape.notes: 400 errors on Grants, Volunteer, and Custom Object endpoints return Array<APIMessage>. Most other endpoints return empty content on 4XX. No documented 429 response body in the OAS3 spec. The ResponseEntity schema is an empty object — no canonical error envelope.

Configuration.CustomObjectMarkerPatternNotes: Neon CRM custom objects are user-defined at runtime. The API exposes /customObjects for the object type definitions and /customObjects/{idOrApiAlias}/records for the actual records. Custom object field definitions are at /customObjects/{idOrApiAlias}/fields. There is no naming convention that marks custom objects in the API — they are enumerated via GET /customObjects. Custom object records use a dynamic data field (type: object) whose schema is only knowable via runtime field discovery.
Configuration.CustomFieldMarkerPatternNotes: Custom field definitions are accessed via the CustomFields tag (/customFields). Custom field values appear on records as accountCustomFields, grantCustomFields, memberShipCustomFieldData, etc. — named arrays of { id, value, name, optionValues } objects. No naming prefix distinguishes them; they are enumerated via GET /accounts/search/outputFields (and similar per-resource search/outputFields endpoints).
Configuration.RateLimitModelNotes: Neon CRM rate limits are defined per-endpoint as a maximum number of simultaneous concurrent requests (thread pool count), NOT as requests per unit time. Most endpoints: limit 5 concurrent requests. POST /accounts/search, POST /donations/search, POST /events/search, POST /orders/search: limit 1 concurrent request. Rate limits are per-organization and per-endpoint independently — simultaneous requests to endpoint A do not count against endpoint B. Exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests.
OutOfScopeObjectFamilies: Webhook delivery (near-real-time signal, not a sync stream)

OutOfScopeObjectFamiliesReason.API v1 / SOAP (legacy): API v1 (documented at developer.neoncrm.com/api/) uses different base URLs, different auth (session-based), and includes SOAP support. It is explicitly superseded by v2. This connector targets API v2 only. API v1 is out of scope to avoid dual-maintenance and because Neon One recommends all new integrations use v2.
OutOfScopeObjectFamiliesReason.Webhook delivery (near-real-time signal,: Webhook delivery (the inbound HTTP payload POSTed to the registered webhook URL) is a near-real-time event signal, not a sync stream. It is not accessible via the API (the client receives it, not queries it). Webhook subscription management (CRUD on /webhooks) IS in scope. Webhook delivery ingestion is out of scope because it requires a publicly reachable endpoint and is architectural infrastructure separate from batch sync.
